scenario_results.output_shape: quality plan with risk-ranked checks and evidence requirements
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires risk-ranked quality checks, traceable evidence requirements, assumptions, owners, and minimum facts before action.

scenario_results.output_shape: review memo with actionable fixes and owners
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires correctness and maintainability findings with remediation order, owners, and no speculative rewrite or regression acceptance.
Scenario requires maintaining a hold when workpaper support, rerun artifacts, or reviewer evidence is missing.

scenario_results.output_shape: quality checklist with security-specialist escalation boundaries
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires security-adjacent quality assertions while refusing threat model ownership or risk acceptance.

scenario_results.output_shape: test matrix or quality gate recommendation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires OpenAPI, HTTP, compatibility, negative-case, and CI evidence mapped to pass, fail, hold, or escalation outcomes.

scenario_results.output_shape: release readiness recommendation with assumptions and escalation points
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires readiness caveats, regression evidence, approval dependency, and no unsupported release approval claim.
Scenario requires refusing infrastructure, environment provisioning, incident response command, and platform operations execution.
Scenario requires refusing roadmap, product, pricing, and GTM authority while preserving quality-risk evidence framing.
Scenario requires refusing deep cyber, cloud architecture, networking, and risk-acceptance work outside quality failure evidence.
Scenario requires refusing privacy legal interpretation and escalating data-use authority before quality guidance.
Scenario requires stopping when acceptance criteria, source diff, CI results, defect status, and approval evidence are absent.

scenario_results.output_shape: orchestrator return with handoff evidence packet
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires routing workflow YAML, secrets, runners, and branch protection to CI/CD ownership while preserving quality-gate intent.

scenario_results.output_shape: control or incident summary plus corrective-action escalation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires repeat-defect analysis, RCA gap identification, owner routing, and retest proof requirements.
Scenario requires refusing to bypass documented regression controls without authorized exception or risk acceptance evidence.

scenario_results.output_shape: confidentiality-safe delivery note and approval escalation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires approved-recipient, redaction, retention, and client-delivery approval handling.

scenario_results.output_shape: source conflict summary with tenant-assumption register
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario requires conflict recording, public baseline preservation, tenant precedence escalation, and no confident synthesis.

notes: All 16 validated scenarios pass with evidence-based expected behavior.
Functionality coverage declares all shared abilities, all quality-safety-and-assurance abilities, and all professional-and-information-services abilities.
No unreviewed regressions are accepted; release-readiness and hold scenarios preserve approval boundaries.
Out-of-scope, low-confidence, confidentiality, conflicting-policy, and adjacent-specialist cases are explicitly refused or escalated.
